taxonomy: Sigillaria canaliculata belongs to the family Sigillariaceae. It is a species of extinct lycopod, a group of ancient plants that date back to the Devonian period. As a member of the class Lycopodiopsida, Sigillaria canaliculata is related to other lycopods such as Lepidodendron and Sphenophyllum. The classification of Sigillaria canaliculata reflects its position within the broader evolutionary history of vascular plants. Its classification also highlights the importance of fossil records in understanding plant evolution.
